steps.title: Spot the contrast that reveals the meaning
steps.content: The word but signals a shift: before, Theia was ______; after, researchers now claim they've identified physical pieces of it. The blank must describe something lacking physical evidence—a state that contrasts with having found actual material.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content: Notional means existing only in theory or concept, not in physical reality—the only choice that fits the sense of something unproven but not disproven. The other three either describe unrelated qualities (desultory), contradict the setup (veritable = genuinely real), or overstate it (spurious = false rather than merely theoretical). This matches choice B — B is correct.
